page: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server optimized for network and Elastic Block Store (EBS) performance. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors, delivering a total of 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and include 35 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It contains no local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

The server features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the m5n.metal demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing, Passmark CPU score, and Passmark CPU encryption. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with strong read-only and write performance for large blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations show strong throughput, alongside high static web serving capabilities. However, single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the average category.

Due to the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, this server is not configured for local storage-heavy tasks or hardware-accelerated machine learning training. This is reflected in its LLM text generation benchmarks, which rank as weak to poor. Qualitatively, the bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and dedicated CPU allocation, making it cost-efficient for workloads that require high network throughput and multi-threaded CPU capacity without the overhead of virtualization. Appropriate workloads include large-scale database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and network-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server optimized for high-bandwidth networking and EBS workloads. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 3.1 GHz clock speed. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, and no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong database and Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance remains average, and LLM text generation benchmarks are weak to poor due to the lack of hardware accelerators. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density, network-heavy deployments. It is best suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation tasks requiring dedicated physical resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and network-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for network-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, network-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and high-speed memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB of DDR4 memory
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Bare-metal architecture with no virtualization overhead
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, resulting in weak LLM text generation
